narration: You've gotten raises. Maybe a few of them. So why does it still feel like you're barely keeping up? There's a name for what's happening to you.
visual: Dark background. Person silhouette at center. Salary badge animates through $45K → $60K → $80K → $100K in green. Empty wallet SVG below stays unchanged. 'WHERE DOES IT GO?' text springs in.

narration: It's called lifestyle creep. Number one: a raise arrives. Number two: spending inflates to match — bigger place, newer car, nicer restaurants. You feel richer for a month, then normal again.
visual: Light background. Vertical timeline: green money bag icon labeled 'RAISE ARRIVES', then red house icon 'BIGGER PLACE', red car icon 'NEWER CAR', red fork/knife icon 'NICER RESTAURANTS'. Each item springs in with numbered badge.

narration: Here's the stat that stings: 75 percent of people upgrade their lifestyle within 90 days of a raise. That extra $500 a month just vanishes — and the investment account stays at zero.
visual: Dark background. Large '75%' number animates counting up. Below: dollar bill SVG splitting — left half flows into spending icons (house, car, coffee cup), right half arrow points to empty piggy bank. 'INVESTMENT ACCOUNT: $0' in red.

narration: Here's the math that'll change how you think about raises forever. Invest that raise delta from age 30 to 65 and you'd retire with $1.4 million extra. That's the actual price of lifestyle creep.
visual: Light background. Two-column comparison: left column 'SPENT ON UPGRADES' shows red downward arrow and flat line. Right column 'INVESTED INSTEAD' shows green exponential curve climbing to '$1,400,000' bold label at top. '35 YEARS' badge between columns.

narration: And here's the wild part — this isn't just a middle-income trap. Americans making $200,000 a year live paycheck to paycheck at nearly the same rate as those making $60,000. More money, same trap.
visual: Dark background. Four salary bars growing taller: $60K, $80K, $100K, $200K. Lifestyle line perfectly tracks each bar, matching it exactly. Savings flatline stays near zero for all four. Label: 'SAVINGS RATE: NEARLY IDENTICAL ACROSS ALL INCOMES'.

narration: Pick one upgrade from your last raise. One. Redirect that exact dollar amount to an index fund automatically. That single habit — repeated with every raise — is how the cycle actually breaks.
visual: Light background. Circle highlights one upgrade item (streaming/coffee). Arrow points from it to a green piggy bank SVG. Piggy bank fills with coins. Bottom CTA badge: 'ONE REDIRECT = $1.4M AT RETIREMENT'. Green checkmark springs in.
